design_principles: Entry points are the external boundary — they translate HTTP requests into application commands and return HTTP responses
No domain logic, invariant enforcement, or workflow orchestration lives in route handlers
Route handlers call use cases exclusively — never repositories, domain objects, or infrastructure adapters directly
Request validation is structural only (Pydantic schema) — semantic validation belongs to the use cases and domain
Exception handlers are registered once at the app level — route handlers never catch use-case exceptions themselves
The container is built once at startup and injected into the router — stateless request handlers share the same wired use case instances
Pydantic schemas (request/response) are defined in the API layer and are independent of use-case command/response dataclasses
HTTP status codes reflect the semantic outcome — 201 for resource creation, 200 for idempotent state transitions, 4xx for client errors

framework.name: FastAPI
framework.rationale: FastAPI is the standard Python framework for AI/ML APIs. It provides async support, automatic OpenAPI documentation, Pydantic-based request validation, and exception handler registration. Minimal boilerplate for three endpoints.
framework.auto_docs: OpenAPI schema served at /docs (Swagger UI) and /redoc

files.purpose: FastAPI application factory. Creates the FastAPI instance, registers exception handlers, builds the container, creates and includes the router. Exposes create_app() for import by the ASGI server entry point.
Defines all three route handlers. Receives the wired AppContainer, builds commands from request schemas, calls use cases, maps responses back to Pydantic schemas.
Pydantic models for all request bodies and response envelopes. Strictly API-layer models — no imports from domain or application layers.
ASGI entry point at workspace root. Imports create_app() and exposes the `app` instance for uvicorn. Single line: app = create_app().

endpoints.name: StartSession
endpoints.summary: Create and initialise a new conversation session for a user
endpoints.description: Creates a ConversationSession, loads user/school/history data, pre-seeds DecisionContext in BC-2, and returns a session_id the client must use for subsequent turn calls. Enforces one active/idle session per user.

fields.name: user_id
fields.note: Pre-resolved trusted user identity. Never authenticated at this boundary.

error_map.message: User already has an active or idle session

endpoints.name: ProcessTurn
endpoints.summary: Submit a user message and receive the assistant's response
endpoints.description: Submits a message to an active session. Runs the full turn pipeline: intent classification, decision intelligence delegation, knowledge retrieval, response generation, and response evaluation. Returns the grounded assistant response. The session must exist and be in idle state.

fields.name: user_id
fields.note: Must match the user_id that created the session

example.message: What products do you have for kids aged 5 to 8?

fields.name: response_text
fields.note: The assistant's grounded response text, or fallback message

fields.name: intent
fields.note: Classified intent: product_inquiry | referral_question | general_question | clarification_response | ambiguous | out_of_scope

fields.name: is_fallback
fields.note: True if quality scores failed and a safe fallback was returned

fields.name: groundedness_score
fields.note: 0.0 – 1.0; how well the response is anchored in retrieved evidence

fields.name: context_precision_score
fields.note: 0.0 – 1.0; fraction of retrieved chunks that are actually relevant

fields.name: context_recall_score
fields.note: 0.0 – 1.0; fraction of relevant information that was retrieved

fields.name: relevance_score
fields.note: 0.0 – 1.0; how directly the response addresses the user's question

example.response_text: We have the Explorer Backpack and the Junior Art Set, both popular with kids in that age range at Lincoln Elementary.

error_map.message: Session is not in a state that accepts turns
User does not own this session

endpoints.name: CloseSession
endpoints.description: Transitions the session to the closed terminal state. Only idle sessions (not mid-turn) can be closed. The requesting user must own the session.

request.note: DELETE body carries user_id for ownership verification. Alternatively can be passed as a query parameter — body is simpler for prototype.

fields.name: user_id
fields.note: Must match the user_id that created the session

error_map.message: User does not own this session
Session cannot be closed in its current state

exception_handler_registry.description: All exception handlers are registered at app level in app.py. Route handlers never catch exceptions. The registration order is: specific exceptions first, then broader base classes.

handlers.note: Raised by both process_turn_use_case and close_session_use_case — same HTTP mapping in both cases

app_startup.description: The FastAPI app is created by the factory function create_app() in app.py. The composition root (build_container) is called once during startup — all use case instances are shared across requests. The ASGI entry point is main.py at the workspace root.
startup_sequence: 1. main.py is loaded by the ASGI server (uvicorn)
2. create_app() is called: build_container() instantiates all infrastructure and use cases
3. Exception handlers are registered on the FastAPI app instance
4. The API router (with all 3 route handlers) is included in the app
5. FastAPI is ready to serve requests

in_scope: Three REST endpoints: POST /sessions, POST /sessions/{session_id}/turns, DELETE /sessions/{session_id}
Pydantic request and response schemas in src/shell/api/schemas.py
FastAPI app factory in src/shell/api/app.py with exception handler registration
Router module in src/shell/api/router.py with three route handler functions
ASGI entry point in main.py at workspace root
Exception-to-HTTP-status mapping for all use-case exceptions
out_of_scope: Authentication / JWT validation — user_id is treated as pre-resolved trusted identity
Rate limiting — not required at prototype scope
CORS configuration — not required unless a browser client is added
GraphQL, CLI, or webhook entry points — REST is the sole delivery channel
Health check endpoint — optional; not required by the deliverable
Background tasks or async processing — pipeline is synchronous
